info.title: WIP Template Store Service
info.description:
## World In a Pie - Template Schema Management

The Template Store service manages document schemas (templates) for the WIP ecosystem.

### Key Features

- **Template Management**: Create and manage document schemas
- **Field Definitions**: Define fields with types, validation, and terminology references
- **Template Inheritance**: Templates can extend other templates
- **Cross-field Validation**: Define rules that validate across multiple fields
- **Reference Validation**: Validate terminology and template references

### Authentication

All endpoints require API key authentication via the `X-API-Key` header.

### Integration

- **Registry Service**: Templates are registered to get unique IDs (UUID by default, configurable per namespace)
- **Def-Store Service**: Terminology references can be validated
- **Namespace Isolation**: Multi-tenant data isolation

post.description:
Create one or more templates.

Each template is registered with the Registry service to get a unique ID.
Namespace is specified per item (default: "wip").
For single items, uses direct creation. For multiple items, uses batch path.

parameters.name: on_conflict

schema.description: How to handle a value collision with an existing template in the same namespace. 'error' (default): treat as error (existing behavior). 'validate': identical schema returns 'unchanged'; compatible (added optional fields only) bumps to version N+1; incompatible returns an error item with error_code='incompatible_schema' and a structured diff.
schema.title: On Conflict

parameters.description: How to handle a value collision with an existing template in the same namespace. 'error' (default): treat as error (existing behavior). 'validate': identical schema returns 'unchanged'; compatible (added optional fields only) bumps to version N+1; incompatible returns an error item with error_code='incompatible_schema' and a structured diff.

get.description:
List templates with pagination.

Supports filtering by status, parent template, and value.
Use latest_only=true to only show the most recent version of each template.

parameters.name: value

schema.description: Filter by template value (shows all versions)
schema.title: Value

parameters.description: Filter by template value (shows all versions)

parameters.name: latest_only

schema.description: Only return latest version of each template
schema.title: Latest Only

parameters.description: Only return latest version of each template

put.description:
Update one or more templates by creating new versions.

The template_id is stable across versions — updates create a new version
document with the same template_id but incremented version number.

delete.description:
Soft-delete one or more templates.

Sets the template status to 'inactive'. Cannot delete templates that
have other templates extending them.
Set force=true per item to delete even if documents exist.

get.description:
Get a template by ID or synonym value.

Accepts canonical UUID or human-readable value (e.g., "PATIENT").
Template IDs are stable across versions. Without a version parameter,
returns the latest version. Returns the template with inheritance resolved
(all fields from parent templates merged).

Get a template by ID without inheritance resolution.

Accepts canonical UUID or human-readable value (e.g., "PATIENT").
Returns the template as stored in the database, without merging
fields from parent templates.

Get the latest version of a template by value.

Returns the template with inheritance resolved.
To get a specific version, use /by-value/{value}/versions/{version}.

parameters.name: namespace

schema.description: Namespace to search in (omit for all)
schema.title: Namespace

parameters.description: Namespace to search in (omit for all)

get.description: Get the latest version of a template by value without inheritance resolution.

Get all versions of a template by value.

Returns all versions sorted by version number (newest first).
This allows viewing the full version history of a template.

parameters.name: namespace

schema.description: Namespace to search in (omit for all)
schema.title: Namespace

parameters.description: Namespace to search in (omit for all)

get.summary: Get Template By Value And Version
get.description:
Get a specific version of a template.

Args:
    value: Template value
    version: Version number

Returns the template with inheritance resolved.

Get dependencies of a template.

Returns information about what depends on this template:
- Templates that extend it
- Documents that use it

Use this to check before deactivating a template.

post.description:
Validate a template's references.

Checks that:
- terminology_ref fields point to existing terminologies in Def-Store
- template_ref fields point to existing templates
- extends points to an existing template

Activate a draft template.

Validates all references and transitions the template from draft to active.
If the template references other draft templates, those are activated too
(cascading activation). All-or-nothing: if any template in the set fails
validation, none are activated.

Use dry_run=true to preview what would be activated without making changes.

Cascade a parent template update to all child templates.

After updating a parent template (which creates a new version), child
templates still extend the old version. This endpoint creates new versions
of all direct children that extend the new parent version.

Only the `extends` pointer is updated — child-specific fields are preserved.

get.description: Get templates that directly extend this template.
Get all templates that extend this template (directly or indirectly).

Health check endpoint.

Verifies MongoDB, Registry, and Def-Store connectivity.

Health check endpoint.

Verifies MongoDB, Registry, and Def-Store connectivity.

Readiness check endpoint.

Returns 200 when the service is ready to accept traffic.

Check referential integrity of template references.

Scans all templates for:
- Orphaned terminology references (referenced terminology not found)
- Orphaned template references (extends, template_ref not found)
- Inactive references (referenced entity is inactive)

Args:
    status: Filter templates by status ('draft', 'active', 'inactive')
    limit: Maximum number of templates to check (default 1000)

Returns:
    IntegrityCheckResult with status, summary, and list of issues

activation_details.title: Activation Details
activation_details.description: Details for each template in the activation set

ActivationDetail.title: ActivationDetail
ActivationDetail.description: Detail for a single template in an activation operation.

details.title: Details
details.description: Structured details for non-error statuses (e.g. compatibility diff for on_conflict=validate)

BulkResultItem.title: BulkResultItem
BulkResultItem.description: Result of a bulk operation for a single item.

CascadeResult.title: CascadeResult
CascadeResult.description: Result of cascading a parent update to a single child template.

value.title: Value
value.description: Value to compare (not needed for exists operators)

template_id.title: Template Id
template_id.description: Pre-assigned template ID (for restore/migration — Registry uses as-is instead of generating)

version.title: Version
version.description: Pre-assigned version (for restore/migration — skips Registry and version computation when used with template_id)

extends_version.title: Extends Version
extends_version.description: Pinned parent version (None = always use latest active parent version)

identity_fields.title: Identity Fields
identity_fields.description: Fields that form the composite identity key

usage.description: Usage class: entity (default), reference, or relationship. Immutable after creation.

source_templates.title: Source Templates
source_templates.description: Template values allowed as edge source (required when usage=relationship; ignored otherwise)

target_templates.title: Target Templates
target_templates.description: Template values allowed as edge target (required when usage=relationship; ignored otherwise)

versioned.title: Versioned
versioned.description: True = updates create new versions; False = overwrite in place. Immutable after creation.

created_by.title: Created By
created_by.description: User or system creating this template

validate_references.title: Validate References
validate_references.description: Validate that terminology_ref and template_ref values exist before creating

CreateTemplateRequest.title: CreateTemplateRequest
CreateTemplateRequest.description: Request to create a new template.

version.title: Version
version.description: Specific version to delete (default: latest for soft-delete, all for hard-delete)

force.title: Force
force.description: Force deletion even if documents exist

DeleteItem.title: DeleteItem
DeleteItem.description: Item in a bulk delete request.

terminology_ref.title: Terminology Ref
terminology_ref.description: Canonical terminology_id for term validation (resolved from value at creation)

template_ref.title: Template Ref
template_ref.description: Canonical template_id for nested template (resolved from value at creation)

reference_type.description: Type of entity being referenced (for reference type)

target_templates.title: Target Templates
target_templates.description: Canonical template_ids for allowed document reference targets (resolved from values at creation)

include_subtypes.title: Include Subtypes
include_subtypes.description: When true, target_templates also accepts documents from child templates (via inheritance)

target_terminologies.title: Target Terminologies
target_terminologies.description: Canonical terminology_ids for allowed term reference targets (resolved from values at creation)

version_strategy.description: How to resolve reference versions (default: latest)

file_config.description: Configuration for file fields (allowed types, size limits)

array_item_type.description: Type of array items (for array type)

array_terminology_ref.title: Array Terminology Ref
array_terminology_ref.description: Canonical terminology_id for array item term validation (resolved from value at creation)

array_template_ref.title: Array Template Ref
array_template_ref.description: Canonical template_id for array item template (resolved from value at creation)

array_file_config.description: File configuration for array items if file type

semantic_type.description: Semantic type for additional validation (email, url, latitude, etc.)

inherited.title: Inherited
inherited.description: Whether this field is inherited from a parent template (set during resolution)

inherited_from.title: Inherited From
inherited_from.description: Template ID of the parent template this field was inherited from

FieldDefinition.title: FieldDefinition
FieldDefinition.description: A field definition within a template.

FieldType.title: FieldType
FieldType.description: Supported field types for template fields.

multiple.title: Multiple
multiple.description: Allow multiple files (field value becomes array of file IDs)

max_files.title: Max Files
max_files.description: Maximum number of files when multiple=true (default: unlimited)

ReferenceType.title: ReferenceType
ReferenceType.description: Types of entities that can be referenced.

sync_enabled.title: Sync Enabled
sync_enabled.description: Whether to sync documents of this template to PostgreSQL

table_name.title: Table Name
table_name.description: Custom PostgreSQL table name (auto-generated from value if not set)

max_array_elements.title: Max Array Elements
max_array_elements.description: Maximum array elements to include when flattening

ReportingConfig.title: ReportingConfig
ReportingConfig.description: Configuration for reporting/analytics sync to PostgreSQL.

SemanticType.title: SemanticType
SemanticType.description:
Universal semantic types that provide meaning beyond base types.

Semantic types add validation and transformation logic for commonly
needed data patterns. They work with base types:
- string: email, url
- number: latitude, longitude, percentage
- object: duration, geo_point

TemplateUsage.title: TemplateUsage
TemplateUsage.description:
How a template's documents are intended to be used.

- entity (default): full document lifecycle, the v1.x behaviour.
- reference: lightweight controlled-vocabulary documents (LOV).
  Reserved for a future phase; currently behaves like entity.
- relationship: typed, property-carrying edge between two
  documents. Requires source_templates / target_templates to be set
  and a source_ref / target_ref reference field on the template.
  See docs/design/document-relationships.md.

extends.title: Extends
extends.description: Parent template ID (changing creates new version)

extends_version.title: Extends Version
extends_version.description: Pinned parent version (None = always use latest active parent version)

updated_by.title: Updated By
updated_by.description: User or system updating this template

UpdateTemplateItem.title: UpdateTemplateItem
UpdateTemplateItem.description: Item in a bulk template update request — includes the ID.

check_terminologies.title: Check Terminologies
check_terminologies.description: Validate terminology references exist in Def-Store

ValidateTemplateRequest.title: ValidateTemplateRequest
ValidateTemplateRequest.description: Request to validate a template's references.

will_also_activate.title: Will Also Activate
will_also_activate.description: Template IDs of other draft templates that would be activated together (draft templates only)

VersionStrategy.title: VersionStrategy
VersionStrategy.description: How references are resolved over time.
